creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Re-reading your reply in #7:

> P.S. I have another machine and it is working with re0 and ath0 on r336659 at startup. (EAP-TLS authentication)

re and ath are PCI devices whereas ue is a USB device handled by devd. Looks like a timing issue.

Regarding:
        laggport: wlan0 flags=0<>   <=== Why is wlan0 a member ?
        laggport: ue0 flags=5<MASTER,ACTIVE>

You should be able to configure lagg0 without wlan0. This suggest your local config must be adding wlan0 to lagg0 either somewhere else in rc.conf or elsewhere like devd.conf or someplace else. Check for rogue adds of wlan0 to lagg0 somewhere in your config. (I tried reproducing this on a real machine and I do this on VMs regularly.)
